reasoning: Q6 execution time is 32 ms vs DuckDB baseline of 19 ms (1.68x gap), with significant room for improvement. No correctness issues or regressions. Multiple concrete, low-to-medium risk optimizations identified that target the core bottleneck: scalar predicate evaluation on 60M rows with 4 independent filters. Predicate reordering (filter bottleneck), SIMD vectorization (CPU bound), and I/O hints (I/O bound) can deliver combined 3-6.5 ms improvement.
focus_areas: Reorder predicate evaluation by selectivity: discount (9%) before shipdate (14.5%) before quantity (48%) to eliminate ~90M unnecessary comparisons
Apply SIMD vectorization (AVX2/AVX-512) to process 4-8 predicate evaluations per instruction, reducing 240M scalar comparisons to ~15M vector operations
Add madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) hints on mmap regions to optimize HDD readahead and sequential access patterns
strategy_notes: Implement predicate reordering first (low risk, quick 1-2 ms win), then add SIMD vectorization (medium risk, 2-3 ms gain). Both work synergistically: reordered predicates benefit from vectorized short-circuit evaluation. Include madvise as a low-cost I/O optimization. Target execution time of 24-28 ms (8-11 ms improvement from current 32 ms), approaching DuckDB's 19 ms baseline.
notes: This is iteration 1 of 3. Aggressive strategy: combine three complementary optimizations to maximize the gap closure within remaining budget. If iteration 1 succeeds, iteration 2 can tackle zone map skipping or other advanced techniques. Monitor for any performance regressions and roll back immediately if predicate evaluation becomes incorrect.
